note: Verbatim phrases of the locked narration, in spoken order, split at the script's <break> tags and sentence boundaries into caption-sized pieces (<=~95 chars). Block windows were measured by cross-correlation of each WC-10-03-sNN.mp3 against the joined master (8 kHz mono PCM). The Sigma(ffprobe audio + 0.4) formula over 10 blocks predicts 168.101s but the ffprobed master is 167.706s (0.395s off, past the +-0.3s gate), so onsets were measured rather than assumed. Sigma(audio) = 164.101s; master - Sigma(audio) = 3.605s ~= 9 x 0.4 -> the master is the 10 blocks concatenated with a 0.4s gap BETWEEN blocks (9 gaps, no trailing gap); the naive x10 formula over-counts one trailing gap. Energy-envelope and fine 1-sample waveform cross-correlation both align every block at corr = 1.000, and every measured onset falls exactly on the 0.08s mp3-frame grid (mp3 encoder-delay padding strips in the PCM concat, so the measured onsets drift progressively behind the naive cumulative sum, reaching -0.38s by s10). Each block startS is its measured onset; endS is the next block's onset; the final block end = ffprobed master duration 167.706s. Hyphenated tokens (I-T, oh-auth, twelve point nine) are the script's TTS pronunciation spellings kept verbatim.

phrases.text: Start in the left rail, under I-T and Support.
The Support group stacks your ticket tools together.
Right below Support Tickets sits Help Desk, Zoho.
One is WashCentral's own queue.
The other is meant to mirror a help desk that lives outside the platform.
Open Help Desk, Zoho.
The frame loads fine.
It is the data that is missing.
Every count across the top shows a dash,
the status and priority filters have nothing to filter,
and the card in the middle says it plainly: Zoho Desk not connected.
The page even hands you the next move, a button straight to its settings.
Head to Settings, Integrations, Zoho Desk.
This is the control room for the connection.
Whether Zoho talks to WashCentral is read from this one page,
so it's where support work on this integration starts.
Look at the header.
A warning badge reads Not Configured.
That's the whole story in two words.
The chips beside it name how the link would work once someone sets it up:
oh-auth, a live view, a server-side token.
Run across the four tiles.
Connection, not set.
Data center, US.
Sync direction, both.
So the tenant has picked how it wants to sync, but no one has authorized the link yet.
Drop into the Credentials group.
Client I-D, blank.
Secret, not set.
Access and refresh tokens, not set.
With nothing stored here, there's no live line to Zoho,
which is exactly why the Help Desk view had nothing to show.
Keep reading down.
Routing, sync, behavior, mapping.
These rules decide where tickets land and which way they flow once the link goes live.
Notice Enabled says Yes while the status still says Not Configured.
Switched on in intent, not yet connected.
Two tabs sit under the header.
Settings, where you are, and Instructions.
The full setup walk-through lives under Instructions, and in lesson twelve point nine.
For today, stay on Settings and just read.
Top right, two buttons.
Test Connection, and Edit Configuration.
Test Connection pings Zoho for real.
Edit Configuration opens the credentials.
Both touch the live integration,
so neither one gets clicked in a read-only check,
or in support unless the change is yours to make.
So, Help Desk, Zoho.
It's an embedded window into an outside help desk,
empty here because the integration is Not Configured.
When a real client's Zoho tickets go quiet, this is the page you open,
and Token Expiry is the first field you read.
You never have to click Test Connection to know where you stand.
